name: Pirates on the Horizon
description: Investigate pirate sightings in the straits and decide whether the rumor is a real threat, a customs misdirection, or the first clue to a larger harbor conspiracy.

stages.description: Learn about the pirate threat from Rashid.
stages.journalEntry: Rashid speaks of pirates spotted near the straits. Ships have gone missing, but the story sounds tangled with harbor rumors and customs talk.

stages.description: Report the pirate sightings to Capitão Rodrigues.
stages.journalEntry: I should inform the fortress garrison about the pirate threat. Capitão Rodrigues will want to know whether this is real piracy or something more convenient to the customs office.

stages.description: Gather more information about the pirates.

objectives.description: Ask Chen Wei about Chinese shipping losses
Ask Aminah what the fishermen say

stages.journalEntry: The Capitão wants more details before he can act. The merchants, fishermen, and dockside runners may have useful information.

stages.description: Decide whether to report, follow the cove lead, or let the rumor settle.
stages.journalEntry: The rumor now points in two directions: the official report, or the harbor side where people keep their real stories. There may also be no clean answer at all.

availablePaths.name: File the Warning
availablePaths.description: Take the concern back into official hands and let the garrison decide the response.

availablePaths.name: Follow the Cove Lead
availablePaths.description: Trace the rumor toward the hidden waterline and see whether the ships are really pirate craft.

availablePaths.name: Let the Rumor Sit
availablePaths.description: Do not stir the city further. Keep the rumor as a quiet warning for later.

stages.description: Return the matter to Rodrigues and let the garrison act on it.
stages.journalEntry: The garrison has the warning. If there is a pirate cove, the city will treat it as a security matter instead of a rumor.
stages.consequenceText: Rodrigues orders a quiet increase in patrols. The waterfront hears that someone has been asking about cargo lists and customs records as well as pirates.

stages.description: Follow up on the lead in Kampung Quarter.
stages.journalEntry: Aminah mentioned that fishermen from the kampung saw a suspicious ship anchored in a hidden cove. Pak Salleh may know whether the sighting belongs to pirates, smugglers, or something tied to the customs books.

cove.text: A ship in a hidden cove is not automatically a pirate ship. It can be a tax dodger, a runner, or a man who pays the right hand and hides from the left.

manifest.text: If the cove and the customs tallies line up, then someone is moving cargo off the books. Pirates or not, the pattern is the same.

stages.journalEntry: I've gathered enough information for the garrison. The pirates may be using a cove south of the city, or the rumor may simply be a mask for customs games. Either way, the next lead points back to the harbor rather than the horizon.

report.greeting: Friend! Have you told the Portuguese about the pirates? They need to know!

pirates.text: Hurry! Every day more ships are at risk. The Capitão commands the garrison - he can send patrols! But if the customs books are involved, the story is never only about pirates.
Pirates, you say? I need more than sailor's tales to mobilize the garrison. Get me details - where were they seen? What ships? Bring me facts, and if customs slippage is part of it, bring that too.
